title: mentha arvensis subsp. borealis
intro: Mentha arvensis subsp. borealis, commonly known as American wild mint or northern wild mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant that is native to North America. This perennial herb has been used for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include its ability to ease digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Mentha arvensis subsp. borealis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, reduce menstrual cramps, and provide relief from cold and flu symptoms. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
